title: Neillia tanakae
taxonomy: Neillia tanakae is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes other flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. It belongs to the genus Neillia, a group of flowering plants native to East Asia. This genus is further divided into several species, with Neillia tanakae being a specific species within it. Neillia tanakae is a type of flowering plant that grows in mountainous regions of East Asia. Its classification as a member of the Rosaceae family is based on its characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Neillia tanakae is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in East Asian cultures. It is used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues. The plant's roots and stems are commonly employed in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, Neillia tanakae has been reported to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential therapeutic applications and benefits.

intro: Neillia tanakae, also known as Japanese Neillia, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neillia tanakae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neillia tanakae is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules.
